name: Fried Apples & Onions
recipeInstructions:
1. Fry the bacon slices in a 12-inch skillet until they are brown and crisp. Set aside on a warm serving platter.
2. While the bacon is frying, peel the onions and cut them into thin slices. To prevent your eyes from watering, hold a slice of bread in your teeth while slicing.
3. Core the apples and cut them crosswise into circles about 1/4 inch thick. Do not peel the skins unless they are tough or scarred, as they add color to the dish.
4. Drain all but 1 tablespoon of fat from the skillet. Add the onion slices and cook over medium-high heat for about 3 minutes.
5. Arrange the apple slices in an even layer on top of the onions. Sprinkle brown sugar over everything and cover the skillet.
6. Cook until the apples are tender, a few minutes longer. Stir gently to prevent scorching.
7. Remove the dish to a warm serving platter with the bacon slices.
This is a "country" dish, often mentioned in conversation but not featured in many cookbooks. Some people believe sugar is essential, while others consider it a "sin." If you share Almanzo's enthusiasm, you might also enjoy trying fried apples and onions with fried potatoes for breakfast.
